sections.question: My question is whether, based on the facts described in this letter, the Fair Political Practices Act would prohibit me from voting upon or participating in any decision by the Malibu City Council to hire as the City Attorney for Malibu an attorney with whom I have had a previous professional affiliation.
sections.question_synthetic: Can a newly elected Malibu City Council member vote on hiring a City Attorney with whom the councilmember's law firm previously served as co-counsel on incorporation litigation and co-counsel on subsequent City of Industry defense litigation, particularly given that the candidate currently serves as City Attorney for the City of Industry?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter dated April 24, 1992, with a May 6, 1992 supplemental correction letter. No FPPC response/advice letter is included in this document. The request contains detailed facts numbered 1-10 describing the professional relationships at issue. The supplemental letter references a correction regarding paragraphs 7 and 10 regarding additional City of Industry work performed in the past month.

embedding.summary: A newly sworn-in Malibu City Council member requests FPPC advice on whether he may participate in a vote to hire a candidate for City Attorney with whom his law firm previously had professional relationships, including serving as co-counsel on incorporation litigation and on City of Industry defense litigation. A supplemental letter corrects the initial submission to note additional recent follow-up work performed for the City of Industry.
